meta.title: Back Room Recovery
meta.description: Final moments before the stage. Alone in the back room with your nerves.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: Music through concrete. Someone laughing outside the door. Your set in less than a minute. The room sounds enormous when you aren't in it.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: One inhale you can own. One exhale nobody gets to monetize.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: The dark doesn't judge. For a moment, you are only the sound of your own breathing and the distant thud of a kick drum that doesn't know your name.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: Hey. It's me. I know you won't hear this tonight. I just needed someone to exist on the other end of something before I walk out there.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: You hit send before you can edit it. Ash will hear it tomorrow. By then it will either be embarrassing or prophetic. No middle ground with voice notes.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: No. Not that line. Too eager. Too bloodless. Again.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: Three attempts on the back of a flyer. The fourth one sticks. You can feel it in your teeth — the opening line that bites back.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: The mirror shows someone who could walk out there and take the room apart. Whether that person exists outside the glass is tonight's open question.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: Good angle. Good lighting. The room already has a version of you — now go out there and make it match.

nodes.speaker: player
nodes.text: You don't look like someone about to be on stage. You look like someone about to be found out. But then — you always have.

nodes.speaker: system
nodes.text: A knock on the door. Not polite. Functional. You're up.
